info.title: Fenergo Nebula Journey Query
info.description:
**Journey Query API**:
Journey Query API provides both configurational and operational methods related to the client Journeys. Use the Journey Command API to create manage and Journey Schemas and Journey Instances. Use the Command Query API to retrieve Journey Schema and Journey Instance aggregates.
**Journey Query API provides**:
  • The ability to query for Journey Schemas.
  • The ability to query for Journey Instances. That includes searching by Entity ID.
  • The ability to calculate lifecycle status for given Entity ID.

parameters.name: X-TENANT-ID
parameters.description: The UiD of the tenant representing organization

200.description: Success. The configuration options

get.description:
Returns journey instance for a given id.

Required permissions:
Following permissions are required: JourneyAccess

parameters.name: journeyInstanceId
parameters.description: Journey instance id

parameters.name: If-None-Match
parameters.description: ETag value from previous API response. Should be used to optimize the short polling. Must be wrapped in double quotes.

parameters.name: X-TENANT-ID
parameters.description: The UiD of the tenant representing organization

200.description: Success. Journey instance is returned

ETag.description: Identifies specific version of Journey Instance. Multiple values are not supported.

messages.message: Not Found. Journey instance with given id does not exist

304.description: The If-None-Match header value is up to date and no journey instance will be returned

Retry-After.description: Specifies the number of second the client should wait before making subsequent API call.

400.description: Bad request. The request has missing/invalid values

401.description: User is not authorized to perform this request

messages.message: Access denied. Following permissions are required: Permission1, Permission2

500.description: Internal server exception. Please, contact your provider.

messages.message: Internal server exception. Please, contact your provider.

businessRelated.description: Collection of business related access layers

fieldName.description: Name of the field to evaluate

dataSource.description: Data source from which field is retrieved. If empty then Entity Data properties will be used.

operation.description: Logical operation to be performed to match the values

logicalOperation.description: Logical operation to be performed on the operands collection

operands.description: Collection of operands for given condition. These might contain additional, nested conditions of type Fenergo.Nebula.Platform.LogicEngine.Model.Dto.ConditionDefinitionDto

valueField.description: Field value used for conditional limit validation

id.description: The ID of the condition

operands.description: Collection of operands for given condition. These might contain additional, nested conditions of type Fenergo.Nebula.Platform.LogicEngine.Model.Dto.ConditionDefinitionDto

id.description: The ID of the condition

operands.description: Collection of operands for given condition. These might contain additional, nested conditions of type Fenergo.Nebula.Platform.LogicEngine.Model.Dto.ConditionDefinitionDto

value.description: Value that should be returned when conditions are met

priority.description: Priority of the value in case when multiple conditional values are evaluated

valueId.description: Value Id that should be returned when conditions are met

isCancellationCommentMandatory.description: Flag indicating if inheritance is enabled or disabled

enableSlaEventNotifications.description: Flag indicating if SLA event notifications are enabled or disabled

restrictTaskReopenToAssignedTeam.description: Flag indicating if task reopening is restricted to users in the task's assigned team

isCancellationReasonMandatory.description: Flag indicating if journey cancellation reason is mandatory

isReassignCommentMandatory.description: Flag indicating if reassign-task comment is mandatory

isReopenReasonEnabled.description: Flag indicating if the reopen-reason dropdown is enabled

isReopenReasonMandatory.description: Flag indicating if the reopen-reason dropdown selection is mandatory

GetJourneyConfigurationResponseDto.description: Request DTO representing current related party access layers inheritance configuration data

messages.description: List of Fenergo.Nebula.Platform.Core.Models.Response.ServiceResponseMessage associated to the service response

workingDays.description: List of working days of the week (e.g. Monday–Friday)

referenceTimezone.description: Reference timezone used for SLA calculations (e.g. "UTC", "Europe/Dublin")

messages.description: List of Fenergo.Nebula.Platform.Core.Models.Response.ServiceResponseMessage associated to the service response

id.description: The UiD of the journey instance

entityDraftId.description: The UiD of the entity draft

entityId.description: The UiD of the entity that journey instance belongs to

journeySchemaId.description: The UiD of the journey schema

journeySchemaVersionNumber.description: The VersionNumber of the journey schema

applicationId.description: The UiD of the grouped journeys

applicationStatus.description: Status of the journey from specific application

applicationType.description: Type of the service that triggered the application

accessLayers.description: Journey instance access layers determining the access to the entity

hasRequiredAccessLayers.description: Flag indicating if current user has required access layers to access this journey instance

jurisdictions.description: Collection of jurisdictions this journey operates on

userTaskPermissions.description:
Per-task permissions for the current user, keyed by task id.
Each entry carries a Fenergo.Nebula.Journey.Query.Application.Dto.PermissionDto per action (e.g. `canReopen`)
with a `permitted` flag and a human-readable `reason` tooltip.
Only emitted when at least one task has a denied permission; otherwise omitted.

originSource.description: The Source that triggered the journey instance creation

originReferenceId.description: The UiD of the origin source that triggered the journey

areReopenCommentsOptional.description: If true then comments are non-mandatory. If false then comments are mandatory.

channelType.description:
Channel type describing whether journeys will be initiated by internal teams (Internal) or external customers through Portal (External)
  • Internal journeys are ones that are initiated and processed by internal teams, e.g., RMs, KYC Analysts, Compliance.
  • External journeys are ones that are initiated and processed by the end customer via the portal.
  • This let's the portal quickly know which journey to pick up and which to ignore.
Might be null for older journey instances that were created before this property was introduced

JourneyInstanceDto.description: Response DTO representing all journey instance data

id.description: The UiD of the Journey Milestone
The UiD of the process

schemaProcessId.description: The UiD of the journey schema process

tasksCompletionOrder.description:
Determines the order of completing tasks. Defaults to Sequential.
One of Sequential, AnyOrder

conditions.description: List of conditions required to trigger process

isConditional.description: True if the process contains conditions, false otherwise

isCompleted.description: True if the process is completed, false otherwise

isSkipped.description: True if the process is skipped, false otherwise

isHidden.description: If true, the process and its contents should not be rendered

committedHours.description: Committed Hours - sets the amount of time a user has to complete a Journey/Stage/Task

dueDate.description: Due Date - calculated date by which Journey/Stage/Task is expected to be completed

approachingDueHours.description: Approaching Due Hours - sets the amount of time when the SLA status change to ‘Approaching Due’

approachingDueDate.description: Approaching Due Date - calculated date after which the SLA status change to ‘Approaching Due’

duePeriod.description: Due Period of Service Level Agreement

pausedPeriodSec.description: Total time in seconds when Journey/Stage/Task was paused

defaultCommittedHours.description: Committed Hours - sets the default amount of time a user has to complete a Journey/Stage/Task

defaultApproachingDueHours.description: Approaching Due Hours - sets the default amount of time when the SLA status change to ‘Approaching Due’

JourneyInstanceServiceLevelAgreementDto.description: Response DTO representing Service Level Agreement of a Journey/Stage/Task, omits Conditions property.

One of Sequential, AnyOrder

conditions.description: List of conditions required to trigger stage

isConditional.description: True if the stage contains conditions, false otherwise

isHidden.description: If true, the stage and its contents should not be rendered

channelType.description:
Channel type describing whether journeys will be initiated by internal teams (Internal) or external customers through Portal (External)
  • Internal journeys are ones that are initiated and processed by internal teams, e.g., RMs, KYC Analysts, Compliance.
  • External journeys are ones that are initiated and processed by the end customer via the portal.
  • This let's the portal quickly know which journey to pick up and which to ignore.

version.description: Version number for given Aggregate (concurrency check)

notes.description: Optional description of the references and details of changes made that will be visible to all users who can access the version. It's assumed that version notes won't contain 'personal identifiable information'. Please note that having many versions with very long notes could hurt API performance in the long term.

areReopenCommentsOptional.description: If true then comments are non-mandatory. If false then comments are mandatory.
